examples: It would be inexpedient to raise taxes further.
To rely on such a measure is inexpedient.
Both domestic and foreign cases concerned indicate that it is usually inexpedient capital mode that leads to the companies "financial difficulties, which sometimes result in their bankruptcy."
Even before Annapolis, Condi Rice has found it inexpedient to do more than mouth platitudes of the kind that once governed George Bush's policies vis a vis the Jewish State and its enemies.
